heading: Forest highways
label: 460lll–14 Forest highways
notes: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (a), was repealed and a new section 204 enacted by , , , 489.Section 204 of title 23Pub. L. 112–141, div. A, title I, § 1119(a)126 Stat. 473July 6, 2012
amendments: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (a), was repealed and a new section 204 enacted by , , , 489.Section 204 of title 23Pub. L. 112–141, div. A, title I, § 1119(a)126 Stat. 473July 6, 2012

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: For purposes of section 204 of title 23, the road known as “The Trace” and every other paved road within the Recreation Area (including any road constructed to secondary standards) shall be considered to be a forest highway.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The States shall be responsible for the maintenance of forest highways within the Recreation Area.

children.heading: Reimbursement
children.label: 2 Reimbursement
children.text: To the maximum extent provided by law, from funds appropriated to the Department of Transportation and available for purposes of highway construction and maintenance, the Secretary of Transportation shall reimburse the States for all or a portion of the costs of maintenance of forest highways in the Recreation Area.
